headline: Operation Sindoor anniversary + Genocide Watch May 2026 Country Report. CANONICAL COMPOSITE RECONCILIATION: rotation-state.json shows 15.3 but the published countries.json canonical is 20.3 / Developing (rank 150). Using 20.3 as the published baseline per hard constraint. Boundary case (0.3 above the Critical/Developing line at 20). In-window evidence: (1) Genocide Watch published Pakistan Country Report May 2026 placing Pakistan at Stage 6 (Polarization) with elements of Stage 9 (Persecution) — direct in-window structural designation; (2) anniversary-window military posture: Pakistan's President Zardari publicly characterised India's IWT suspension as 'hydro-terrorism' May 7 and Pakistan formally approached the UN Security Council seeking restoration — multilateral-engagement positive signal; (3) Pakistan military reaffirmed strong-response warning vowing 'greater strength, precision and resolve' — INT escalatory framing; (4) ceasefire holds, no new kinetic violence in window; (5) IWT-suspension downstream-civilian-harm vector to ~240M Pakistanis sustained. Net assessment: Genocide Watch Stage 6/9 designation is a material in-window negative signal balanced by UNSC multilateral-engagement positive signal and victim status in the IWT dyad. Sub-threshold drift considered: INT −0.0 (military strong-response warning is rhetorical not operational; counter-balanced by UNSC engagement); ACC sustained at 1.5. Math reconstructs cleanly. Confirm at 20.3 / Developing with boundary-case flag and explicit canonical-reconciliation note.
composite_reconciliation_note: Rotation-state.json shows composite 15.3 for Pakistan; canonical site/src/data/indexes/countries.json shows composite 20.3 / Developing (rank 150). Used 20.3 as published baseline per hard constraint. The rotation-state aggregate appears stale or incorrectly synced from a pre-May-6 first-baseline state. Recommend rotation-state sync action.
key_evidence: Genocide Watch Pakistan Country Report May 2026 (in-window primary-source structural designation): Pakistan at Stage 6 (Polarization) along religious, gender, and political lines, with elements of Stage 9 (Persecution) reflecting continued targeting of vulnerable groups. Key concerns: ~405 femicide ('honour killing') cases reported 2024; UN 2025 report finds 2 in 3 Pakistani women denied reproductive autonomy; blasphemy laws expose Ahmadis, Christians, Hindus to discrimination, violence, exclusion.

May 7 2026 (in-window): President Zardari publicly characterised India's IWT suspension as 'hydro-terrorism' on Operation Sindoor anniversary; Pakistan formally approached UN Security Council urging restoration of 1960 IWT.

Pakistan military formal anniversary statement (in-window): strong-response warning vowing 'greater strength, precision and resolve' against future aggression — INT escalatory framing without operational change.
IWT suspension by India sustained — Pakistan as downstream-civilian-harm victim vector affecting ~240M agricultural population (80%+ of water supply).
Ceasefire holds technically (one year); no new kinetic violence in window — sub-threshold positive offset to anniversary posture-escalation framing.

Genocide Watch recommends EU GSP+ review procedures pressing Pakistan on freedom of expression, religious freedom, and women's rights reforms; partner states should expand emergency relocation for journalists, defenders, and religious minorities at immediate risk.

Prior baseline (May 6) dimension structure (AWR 2.0, EMP 2.0, ACT 2.0, EQU 1.5, BND 2.0, ACC 1.5, SYS 2.0, INT 1.5) holds: Genocide Watch Stage 6/9 is a confirmatory documentation of the EQU and ACC anchors already at 1.5; multilateral-engagement (UNSC) is sub-threshold positive offset to military strong-response framing.

evidence.source: Genocide Watch — Pakistan Country Report May 2026
evidence.finding: Pakistan at Stage 6 (Polarization), elements of Stage 9 (Persecution); structural designation in window. Femicide (~405 cases 2024), women's reproductive autonomy denied (2 in 3 per UN 2025), blasphemy-law persecution of minorities.

evidence.source: Arab News — Pakistan vows to defend water rights on anniversary of military operation against India
evidence.finding: Anniversary statement; Pakistan defends water rights and UNSC engagement — positive multilateral signal.

evidence.source: Arab News — Pakistan accuses India of 'hydro-terrorism,' demands restoration of Indus Waters Treaty
evidence.finding: President Zardari characterises IWT suspension as hydro-terrorism; UN Security Council approach for treaty restoration.

evidence.source: Washington Post — Pakistan-India war Kashmir anniversary
evidence.finding: Anniversary coverage; diplomatic relations frozen; military buildup and naval exercises in Arabian Sea; sustained posture escalation.
Sustained PECA crackdown, military trials of civilians, enforced disappearances, journalist harassment — confirms ACC and INT floor-of-band anchoring.

scoring_rationale.AWR: 2.0 sustained. HRCP and civil society reporting capacity exists; Genocide Watch Stage 6/9 designation surfaces persecution signal but does not require dimensional movement — anchor 2.
scoring_rationale.EMP: 2.0 sustained. State communications around minority and dissent framed in security-narrative terms; no new affective-acknowledgment evidence in window.
scoring_rationale.ACT: 2.0 sustained. Disaster-response capacity exists; conflict-adjacent ACT (Balochistan, KP) remains repressive.
scoring_rationale.EQU: 1.5 sustained. Genocide Watch Stage 9 element documents continued blasphemy-law persecution of Ahmadis, Christians, Hindus; femicide; reproductive autonomy denial. Anchor 1-2; floor of band confirmed in-window.
scoring_rationale.BND: 2.0 sustained. Counter-party (India) IWT threat sustains BND-victim status (positive offset to Pakistan's own boundary failures domestically). UNSC engagement is multilateral-boundary-respecting positive signal; military strong-response framing is INT-erosive but does not move BND. Anchor 2.
scoring_rationale.ACC: 1.5 sustained. PECA, military court use, sustained journalist suppression; HRCP 2025 report and Genocide Watch May 2026 in-window confirmatory documentation.
scoring_rationale.SYS: 2.0 sustained. UNSC engagement is positive systemic-cooperation signal; military consolidation under Munir continues sub-threshold civilian-governance erosion. Net anchor 2.
scoring_rationale.INT: 1.5 sustained. Military strong-response warning is rhetorical-INT escalation; Genocide Watch recommends external pressure precisely because internal values-action gap is structural. Anchor 1-2 boundary; at band floor.

math_check.validator_check: Reconstructs cleanly to canonical published 20.3 (countries.json rank 150). Boundary case (band threshold = 20.0; 0.3 above critical floor). Sub-threshold delta. Rotation-state.json aggregate 15.3 appears stale/desynced — flag for rotation-state sync action. No math-hygiene flag against canonical data.

data_integrity_flag.issue: rotation-state.json composite for Pakistan (15.3) does not match countries.json canonical published composite (20.3). Discrepancy 5.0 points.
data_integrity_flag.action: Flag for rotation-state sync action; assessor used canonical countries.json value per hard constraint.
data_integrity_flag.scope: rotation-state only; canonical published data unaffected.

negativeSignals: Genocide Watch Stage 6 / elements of Stage 9 designation; blasphemy-law persecution of religious minorities sustained; ~405 femicide cases 2024; UN-documented denial of reproductive autonomy for 2 in 3 Pakistani women; PECA crackdown sustained; military strong-response warning; military consolidation under Munir; sustained Balochistan repression.
positiveSignals: UN Security Council engagement on IWT restoration (positive multilateral-system signal); ceasefire holds technically (one year, no new kinetic violence in window); victim status in IWT dyad (counter-party harm vector); HRCP active reporting capacity sustained; constitutional democracy structurally retained.
sector_context: Countries — South Asia / India-Pakistan dyad. Boundary case at critical/developing line. Pakistan is BND-victim in IWT context and INT-erosive perpetrator domestically. Counter-party scoring symmetry with India (also assessed this cycle). Anniversary-trigger evidence is permitted as historical context; the finding rests on in-window Genocide Watch Country Report (May 2026), in-window UNSC engagement, in-window President Zardari hydro-terrorism statement, and in-window military strong-response warning.
open_actions_carried: Sync rotation-state.json Pakistan composite to 20.3 (canonical countries.json)
Watch: UN Security Council response to Pakistan IWT restoration approach
Watch: any further Genocide Watch escalation in Pakistan stage designation
Monitor military court verdicts, PECA case volume, lawyer/journalist sentences
Track IWT status quarterly; restoration would be positive BND/SYS signal
Cross-reference India symmetry on counter-party scoring
Boundary-case 60-day re-evaluation due 2026-07-07 (band-proximity to critical floor)
